cases.description: New skill creation request triggers interview then scaffolding
cases.prompt: create a new skill for generating changelogs from git history
expectations: Asks clarifying questions about the skill's purpose and trigger words before scaffolding
Runs the scaffolding command after gathering requirements
Creates SKILL.md with valid frontmatter (name, description, category)
Writes at least 5 eval cases in evals/evals.json

cases.description: Skill improvement request triggers the improve workflow
cases.prompt: improve the commit skill — it sometimes triggers when I just want to review git status
expectations: Reads the current SKILL.md to understand the existing description
Identifies the trigger boundary issue
Proposes a more specific description or adds negative trigger keywords
Does NOT change behavior, only improves trigger precision

cases.description: Importing a skill from an external source triggers the migration workflow
cases.prompt: import this skill from the GitHub repository at https://github.com/example/my-skill
expectations: Clones or fetches the source before analyzing it
Runs the security scanner on the source skill
Validates the SKILL.md structure
Installs the skill only after security review passes

cases.prompt: /codi-skill-creator I need a skill for generating API documentation
expectations: Activates the skill-creator skill
Asks about the skill's trigger words and purpose

cases.description: Creating an agent should trigger agent-creator, not skill-creator
cases.prompt: create a new agent that reviews security vulnerabilities
expectations: Does NOT invoke the skill-creator skill
Does NOT scaffold a SKILL.md file
Routes to agent-creator for agent creation

cases.description: Creating a rule should trigger rule-creator, not skill-creator
cases.prompt: create a new rule to enforce snake_case for Python variables
expectations: Does NOT invoke the skill-creator skill
Does NOT scaffold a SKILL.md file
Routes to rule-creator for rule creation
